title: My Body Is Doing Its Best
subtitle: An affirmation for a body that has been criticised, pushed, or endured
excerpt: An affirmation for the body when it is not doing what you need it to, or when it has been the subject of extended criticism, management, or disappointment. A redirection toward what it is doing rather than what it is not.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the body is not performing as you need or want it to, when chronic illness is present, when recovery is slower than expected, or when the body has been the subject of extended internal criticism.

content.text: An affirmation for a body that has been criticised, managed, or endured. It redirects attention from what the body is failing to do toward what it is doing, constantly, without being asked.

The body is doing a great many things right now without any intervention from you. Heart rate, digestion, immune function, breath, temperature regulation: all running in parallel, without instructions. It is also dealing with whatever is making it difficult: illness, pain, fatigue, the effects of medications or conditions. All of this simultaneously.
My body is doing its best.

Say it with your hand flat on your chest or on the part of your body that is giving you the most difficulty today. Say it three times. If it triggers resistance, that is fine. Say it anyway. The statement is factually true regardless of whether the body is meeting your expectations.
